statement: The remaining live-loop instability before the next reset is dominated by batch apply brittleness, projection timeout pressure, and overly broad cohort status reads rather than worker readiness or missing workflow bindings.
rationale: The live cohort remained healthy-progressing with no stuck items while score persistence lagged provider-complete batches. That points to reconciliation/projection/read-path weakness, not a dead worker or broken workflow start path.
keyAssumptions: The captured campaign snapshot accurately reflects the live cohort at audit time.
The observed score lag is caused by post-provider apply/reconciliation behavior rather than a different unseen provider failure.
validationCriteria: Cohort snapshot remains healthy-progressing during the audit window.
Repo-side fixes improve retries/timeouts/status reads without requiring another architectural change.
critiquePoints: A future failure could still expose a missing durable reconciler after provider completion.
questions: Should a dedicated chunk-level batch reconciliation debug surface land before the next reset?
